verses.text: When the feast ends, the people go out and smash the images, cut down the groves, and throw down the high places throughout Judah and beyond. Worship overflows into reform. True devotion does not stay in the temple but goes home to tear down every idol.

Hezekiah restores the priestly courses, gives his own portion for the offerings, and commands the people to provide the priests' due, 'that they might be encouraged in the law of the LORD.' He frees God's servants to serve. Those who teach should be supported, that they may give themselves wholly to the work.

He frees God's servants to serve. Those who teach should be supported, that they may give themselves wholly to the work.

Hezekiah restores the priestly courses, gives his own portion for the offerings, and commands the people to provide the priests' due, 'that they might be encouraged in the law of the LORD.' He frees God's servants to serve. Those who teach should be supported, that they may give themselves wholly to the work.

The people respond by bringing firstfruits and tithes in such abundance that they pile up in heaps, and the leaders bless the LORD and His people. Generosity answers a call to give. When hearts are right with God, His house is supplied to overflowing.

The people respond by bringing firstfruits and tithes in such abundance that they pile up in heaps, and the leaders bless the LORD and His people. Generosity answers a call to give. When hearts are right with God, His house is supplied to overflowing.

Hezekiah asks about the heaps, and Azariah answers that since the people began to bring offerings, 'we have had enough, and have left plenty: for the LORD hath blessed his people.' Giving did not impoverish but enriched them. God repays open hands with abundance and to spare.

Hezekiah asks about the heaps, and Azariah answers that since the people began to bring offerings, 'we have had enough, and have left plenty: for the LORD hath blessed his people.' Giving did not impoverish but enriched them. God repays open hands with abundance and to spare.

Hezekiah prepares storerooms and appoints faithful overseers to manage the offerings. The abundance is handled with order and care. God's provision deserves wise stewardship; gifts freely given should be faithfully kept and justly distributed.

Hezekiah prepares storerooms and appoints faithful overseers to manage the offerings. The abundance is handled with order and care. God's provision deserves wise stewardship; gifts freely given should be faithfully kept and justly distributed.

The offerings are distributed faithfully to the priests in their cities, to every male from three years old, by their genealogies. Each is given his due in good order. Faithfulness in administration honours God as truly as zeal in worship.

The offerings are distributed faithfully to the priests in their cities, to every male from three years old, by their genealogies. Each is given his due in good order. Faithfulness in administration honours God as truly as zeal in worship.

Provision is made for the priests and Levites by their families, the small and the great alike, wherever they dwell. None is overlooked. God's care reaches every servant, and those who order His house must see that all are fairly supplied.

Provision is made for the priests and Levites by their families, the small and the great alike, wherever they dwell. None is overlooked. God's care reaches every servant, and those who order His house must see that all are fairly supplied.

In all this Hezekiah did what was good and right and true before the LORD, and 'in every work that he began... he did it with all his heart, and prospered.' Wholehearted service is the key. God prospers the one who serves Him not by halves but with all his heart.
